name: FERC Order 889
description: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission guidelines on information sharing and electric transmission interfaces.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - OASIS Access Control Roster
questions.text: Are OASIS information system access rights limited to authorized users (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Review user access provisioning logs, check active directory profiles, and audit router access lists.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the open access governance configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical FERC 889 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - Transmission Link Vetting
questions.text: Are external transmission links verified and authorized annually (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Audit external communications crossings and boundary data flows for transmission nodes.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the open access governance configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical FERC 889 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - OASIS Cryptography Controls
questions.text: Is cryptographic encryption enforced for all OASIS data transit (utilizing VPN tunnels, encrypted Modbus/DNP3 secure protocols, and HSM keys)?
questions.description:
Verify transmission cryptographic settings, check VPN configurations, and review parameters.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the data link encryption configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Traversing industrial telemetry in cleartext across converged networks invites eavesdropping and packet injection. Malicious actors can execute Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks, spoofing HMI screens while sending dangerous control commands.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - Hardware Key Enclave Management
questions.text: Are cryptographic keys managed in secure hardware enclaves (utilizing VPN tunnels, encrypted Modbus/DNP3 secure protocols, and HSM keys)?
questions.description:
Verify secure hardware-backed cryptographic modules (HSM) protecting operational credentials.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the data link encryption configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Traversing industrial telemetry in cleartext across converged networks invites eavesdropping and packet injection. Malicious actors can execute Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks, spoofing HMI screens while sending dangerous control commands.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - OASIS Boundary Segment Separation
questions.text: Are OASIS system components segregated in dedicated subnetworks (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Ensure DMZ isolation, verify router ACL configurations, and audit reverse-proxy configurations.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the perimeter segregation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - Boundary Traffic Monitoring
questions.text: Is boundary traffic monitored for Smart Grid anomaly detection in real-time (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Inspect IDS status reports, signature update frequency, and network sensor alarm registers.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the perimeter segregation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - OASIS Backup Isolation
questions.text: Are daily backups of OASIS configurations secured and tested?
questions.description:
Review backup logs, verify secure isolated offsite replica storage, and check test reports.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the operations continuity configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - Disaster Recovery Failover Plan
questions.text: Is an active energy infrastructure disaster recovery failover plan tested annually?
questions.description:
Review failover test logs, check backup server registers, and inspect procedures.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

Inspect the operations continuity configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: FERC Order 889 - OASIS Incident Reporting Plan
questions.text: Is a cyber security incident reporting plan formally defined and maintained (aligned with incident response playbooks, offsite backups, and isolated write-once media)?
questions.description:
Review reporting trees, emergency phone trees, and regulatory notification frameworks.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the incident reporting control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Failing to maintain isolated, offline backups during convergence events risks catastrophic downtime during ransomware outbreaks. If backups reside on the shared enterprise domain, the same malware that encrypts SCADA HMIs will wipe the recovery configurations.

questions.description:
Check vendor contracts, review coordination procedure guides, and verify contact lists.
